_note: Manual in-game crafting difficulty-color observations, used to reverse-engineer recipe trivials the wiki is missing (it shows '?'). Difficulty colors easiest->hardest: green (= trivial, no more skill-ups), lightblue, darkblue, white (~50% success), yellow, orange, red. A recipe seen GREEN at skill S has trivial <= S; any other color means trivial > S. Recording the same recipe at several skill levels pins the exact trivial (it turns green at its trivial) and calibrates each colour's skill-gap. Edit this file as you hit new skill milestones; the site merges it over the wiki recipe data.

Blacksmithing.note: All copper recipes not listed here were green/trivial at skill 48. Enchanted variants matched their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: +2 skill from the level-48 reading. EVERY recipe moved exactly one colour step easier, so a colour band is worth ~2 skill points in this range (both data points agree). The level-48 lightblue recipes are now green (trivial), pinning their trivial at ~49-50. Enchanted variants still match their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

trivialEstimates._method: Derived from the colour of each recipe at skill 48 AND skill 50. Every recipe shifted exactly one colour step easier over those +2 skill, so each colour band is ~2 skill points wide here; estimates are +/-1-2 and tighten with each new milestone reading. Recipes still RED at skill 50 are a lower bound only until a higher-skill reading shows them shift colour.
